title: Comparison-sort lower bound: height >= lg(n!) (TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.3.1)
statement_informal: A comparison sort is a binary decision tree that must distinguish all n! orderings, so it has >= n! leaves. Since a binary tree of height h has <= 2^h leaves, its height (worst-case comparisons) is >= log2(n!) = Omega(n log n). Promotes decision-tree-leaves-bound to the actual lg(n!) bound.

cost_model.notes: height of the decision tree = worst-case comparisons. fact n <= leaves t (must distinguish n! orderings) and leaves t <= 2^height t give log2(n!) <= height t via Nat.log2_le_mono + Nat.log2_pow2.

references: Knuth, TAOCP Vol. 3 §5.3.1 — the information-theoretic lower bound: any comparison sort needs at least ceil(lg n!) comparisons in the worst case.
